title: Ordering at a Cafe
learningGoal: The learner will write a polite request for a cafe item, combining Katakana nouns with Hiragana expressions.
instructions: Imagine you are at a Japanese cafe. You want to order a 'Juice' and an 'Ice Cream'. Write a single polite sentence asking for these. Since we haven't learned the word for 'and' yet, just pick ONE item to order for this exercise (e.g. 'Ice Cream, please').

contentItems.text: When ordering, you don't need a complex sentence. Just state the item in Katakana, followed by a comma (、), and then the polite request phrase in Hiragana.

writingPrompt: Write a polite order for 'Ice Cream'. Use the exact spelling for Ice Cream in Katakana, followed by the polite request phrase.
promptGoal: To construct a natural, common service interaction using a loan word.
referenceHints: Start with 'Excuse me' to get the waiter's attention (optional but good).
Write 'Ice Cream' exactly as it appears in the word bank.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: すみません。アイスクリーム、おねがいします。
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfectly polite and uses all correct scripts (Hiragana and Katakana mixed properly).

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: アイスクリーム、おねがいします。
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Correct and totally acceptable, though omitting 'sumimasen' makes it slightly more abrupt.

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the learner write 'Aisukuriimu' correctly with the elongation mark?
Did the learner use 'onegaishimasu' at the end?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: アイスクリーム、おねがいします。
acceptableAlternates: すみません。アイスクリーム、おねがいします。

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You can now successfully order an item at a Japanese cafe.
feedbackRules.onFail: Make sure you include the item name and 'onegaishimasu'. Look closely at how 'Aisukuriimu' is spelled.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good attempt! Pay close attention to the elongation mark (ー) in Katakana words.

hintText: Don't forget the dash (ー) in Aisukuriimu. It is located right after 'ri'.
